summary: Retire the reachability dial â€” the assembly `reachability` fact and its severity opt-in; 40-composition: there is no fifth field

per.section: Decision: the reachability dial retires into the clause algebra

edit.description: Delete `reachability_from_declarations` (main.rs:1141) and its call (main.rs:567), and the `if let Some(reachability) = assembly_reachability { â€¦ }` opt-in block that builds the activation map and calls `graph::reachable` (main.rs:726-747). 40-composition: reachability is demanded by an ordinary edge-scope clause, not a dedicated field. Keep `pub fn graph::reachable` (graph.rs:370 â€” a live capability, still exercised by tests/graph.rs; a pub lib fn used only by an integration test draws no dead-code warning) â€” only its dial-driven opt-in goes. Sweep the dial's narrating comments: the facts-gather line (main.rs:554, drop 'â€¦and the reachability') and the opt-in preamble (main.rs:720-726). LEAVE main.rs:629's directive-classing analogy comment ('a pure fact, not a graph-scope opinion like reachability') â€” a different feature; reachability-as-clause keeps that contrast conceptually intact.

Delete the `Harness.reachability` field â€” `readonly reachability?: Severity` (assembly.ts:35), its init-param twin `reachability?: Severity` (:48), and the passthrough `reachability: init.reachability` (:55). The assembly is four fields: members Â· expect Â· require Â· settings. Fix the header TSDoc naming reachability as a fifth field (assembly.ts:3-4: `members Â· expect Â· require Â· settings Â· reachability` â†’ drop `Â· reachability`).

Delete the `if (harness.reachability !== undefined) facts.push({ fact: "reachability", value: harness.reachability })` emission in `assemblyFactRows` (declarations.ts:151-152) and the doc line naming 'reachability when the dial is set' (declarations.ts:146). Simplify the AssemblyFactRow header doc (declarations.ts:43: 'authority, reachability, or an edge' â†’ 'authority or an edge'). The AssemblyFactRow shape stays (authority + edge facts survive; the row always opens with the authority fact at :150). No SEAM_VERSION bump â€” dropping an optional fact the Rust reader now ignores (it matched only on `fact == "reachability"`, deleted with `reachability_from_declarations`).

Sweep the now-stale AssemblyFactRow doc comments naming `reachability` as an assembly-fact discriminator â€” drift.rs:922 ('authority, reachability, edges' â†’ 'authority, edges'), :1068 ('(`authority`, `reachability`, `edge`)' â†’ '(`authority`, `edge`)'), :1072 ('`authority`, `reachability`, or `edge`' â†’ '`authority` or `edge`'), :1074 ('an `authority`/`reachability` fact carries' â†’ 'an `authority` fact carries'). Doc-only: the struct is generic (`fact: String`) and unchanged â€” it still tolerates an old lock's reachability row by simply not matching it. No other open entry touches drift.rs, so this sweep is parallel-safe.

tests.asserts: the `reachable` library predicate still holds over synthetic graphs (capability retained; only the assembly dial retired)

acceptance: `rg -n 'reachability_from_declarations|assembly_reachability' src/` returns nothing and `rg reachability sdk/src/assembly.ts sdk/src/declarations.ts` returns nothing (fieldÂ·paramÂ·passthroughÂ·emissionÂ·both doc lines all gone; kind.ts's graph-reachability registration doc untouched); the assembly is four fields; `pub fn reachable` survives in src/graph.rs with tests/graph.rs green; cargo test + `pnpm --dir sdk test` green.
notes: Blocker PKG-NOUN-SDK-COLUMN shipped (534828d) â†’ open, the one disjoint slice. Retires only the assembly dial; reachability-as-edge-scope-clause (the corpus replacement, 40-composition) rides the clause-algebra growth downstream of REQUIREMENT-CLAUSES-RECUT â€” deferred, not this cut. Rewrite vs prior tick added drift.rs (stale discriminator docs) + a precise acceptance (the old `fact.*reachability` regex false-matched surviving reachability-concept prose).

summary: Recut the requirement to `meansÂ·kindÂ·requiredÂ·clauses?Â·verifiedBy?` â€” retire count/unique/membership/degree facets for a clauses array, across engine + SDK + seam

per.section: Decision: set-scope demands are clauses â€” no noun enumerates the algebra

gate.reason: frame-scale and cross-language: the four facets live in compose.rs (Requirement + CountBound/Membership/DegreeBound), drift.rs RequirementRow columns, roster.rs (the set-scope check), AND sdk/contract.ts (:83-86, verified on disk this tick), and moving them onto a `clauses` array reshapes ClauseRow/engine dispatch to carry set/edge-scope predicates and bumps SEAM_VERSION on both sides (tests/emit.rs runs the real SDK). Needs a decomposition ceremony (John + session) to cut the serialized chain before parallel-safe slices, like GATE-READ-LOCK-DEMOLITION got. Also resolves whether reachability-as-clause and the held general-degree-band predicate ride this same clause-algebra growth.

edit.description: The Requirement interface enumerates `count?`/`unique?`/`membership?`/`degree?` (contract.ts:83-86) and lacks `clauses?` â€” the facet spelling 10-contracts rejects. Placeholder anchor; the ceremony scopes the full cross-language chain (compose.rs/drift.rs/roster.rs/engine.rs + sdk/contract.ts/declarations.ts + SEAM_VERSION).

acceptance: A requirement carries set-/edge-scope demands as ordinary clause values (each with its own severity/guidance/cite), not named facet fields; the SDK Requirement type reads `meansÂ·kindÂ·requiredÂ·clauses?Â·verifiedBy?`; the lock's requirement row carries no facet columns; SEAM_VERSION bumped in lockstep.
notes: Parked for a decomposition ceremony â€” the Rust facets in compose/drift/roster + the SEAM bump make it a serialized chain, not a single disjoint slice. Do not file the naive single entry. Downstream of it: reachability-as-edge-scope-clause (the REACH-DIAL replacement).

summary: Ship the three acquisition channels â€” SDK npm package, engine binary pinned via per-platform optionalDependencies + standalone assets, the plugin/bundle â€” fail-loud on a missing platform binary

gate.reason: needs the per-platform engine-binary release workflow build-out + John's decide-at-release calls (optional Apple notarization for the standalone mac binary, USPTO name screen) before the mechanical wrappers validate end-to-end. No marketplace/signing creds exist â€” a marketplace is a git repo + marketplace.json, community submission a free web form gated by `claude plugin validate` (verified 2026-07-06, code.claude.com/docs); npm creds done 07-05. SDK scope resolved (`@dtmd/temper` 0.0.3, single package, `(sdk-package-layout)`); channel 1's SDK foundation shipped

new.description: Build the per-target release binaries and publish them to the npm platform packages (channel 2: the engine binary pinned by the SDK via per-platform `optionalDependencies`, plus standalone GitHub release assets), and produce/publish the plugin bundle with its `marketplace.json` (channel 3). Channel auto-detected. Distinct from any CI *check* job, which is a documented two-line user-authored job (`temper check` + `emit --frozen` byte-compare), not a generated file (50-distribution).

edit.description: The root `package.json` today is the *private* flume control-plane manifest (`temper-flume-harness`, `private: true`). The npm distribution entry needs platform-specific `optionalDependencies` pointing at per-target prebuilt-binary packages plus a launcher that resolves and execs the platform binary, erroring loudly if none is installed (fail-loud, 50-distribution). Reconcile with the flume manifest rather than clobbering it. NB distinct from sdk/package.json (`@dtmd/temper`, already published â€” the engine pin is declared there, once).

acceptance: A stranger acquires temper via the SDK's pinned engine binary (per-platform optionalDependencies), standalone release assets, or the plugin/bundle (marketplace.json); a missing platform binary is an install error, never a silent skip (50-distribution 'Fail-loud delivery').
notes: Release infra, mostly outside src/; cargo gates can't validate npm/bundle publish; needs human creds. ch1 = SDK npm (`@dtmd/temper`, published 0.0.3); ch2 = engine binary via per-platform optionalDependencies + standalone; ch3 = plugin/bundle via `temper bundle` + marketplace.json. Parked on human release creds + the engine-binary workflow. USPTO name screen due before launch.
